title: Intel names a CPU constraint only when scoped to the processor
content: Phrases such as Intel CPU, Intel processor, 英特尔处理器, or CPU 用 Intel constrain the CPU brand. They do not imply an Intel graphics card.

title: Ryzen and scoped AMD language identify an AMD CPU
content: Ryzen or 锐龙 unambiguously identifies an AMD CPU. Generic AMD should only become a CPU constraint when processor context is present.

title: NVIDIA aliases belong to the GPU scope
content: NVIDIA, GeForce, RTX, 英伟达, and N卡 identify an NVIDIA graphics preference. They must not alter CPU brand selection.
patterns: 显卡\s*(?:要|用|选|选择)?\s*(?:nvidia|英伟达|rtx)

title: Radeon and A卡 identify an AMD GPU
content: Radeon, RX graphics, A卡, or AMD explicitly scoped to a graphics card identify an AMD GPU. Generic AMD without context is ambiguous and should not set both CPU and GPU.

title: Intel only means GPU when Arc or graphics context is explicit
content: Intel Arc, Arc GPU, or 英特尔显卡 identifies an Intel GPU. The word Intel next to CPU must never set the GPU brand.
patterns: 显卡\s*(?:要|用|选|选择)?\s*(?:intel|英特尔|arc)

title: Pure white is a strong visible-component requirement
content: 纯白, 全白, or all-white means visible components should use white variants when the catalog has them. Prioritize the case, graphics card, motherboard, memory, and cooler; hidden internal parts must not compromise safety merely to match color.

title: Ordinary white wording is an appearance preference
content: 白色主机, 白色配色, or white build is normally a preferred aesthetic rather than permission to violate compatibility or overspend. Visible components matter most.
patterns: 白色(?:主机|机箱|配色|主题)

title: Black build language is an appearance preference
content: 纯黑 or black build describes the visible color theme. Compatibility and cooling remain higher priority than hidden-component color.
patterns: 黑色(?:主机|机箱|配色|主题)

title: No RGB is an exclusion constraint
content: 不要 RGB, 无光, or no RGB means illuminated variants should be excluded when possible. It does not mean the system must use black parts.

title: RGB is a lighting preference, not a compatibility rule
content: RGB or 灯效 requests lighting on visible components. It may guide case, memory, cooler, and fan selection, but must never override socket, clearance, cooling, or PSU safety.

title: No liquid cooling is a hard exclusion
content: 不要水冷, 风冷 only, or no liquid cooling excludes AIO and custom-loop coolers. The engine must then verify that an eligible air cooler can handle the selected CPU.

title: Liquid cooling language prefers an AIO
content: 水冷 or AIO requests a liquid cooler. Radiator support and CPU cooling capacity remain deterministic compatibility checks.

title: Quiet means acoustic optimization across several categories
content: 静音 or 安静 should influence case airflow, cooler headroom, GPU efficiency, and PSU load margin together. A single quiet-tagged part does not make the whole system quiet.

title: Small-form-factor language changes multiple hard constraints
content: 小钢炮, SFF, ITX, or 小机箱 implies Mini-ITX or similarly compact enclosure intent. It tightens motherboard form factor, PSU form factor, GPU clearance, and cooler clearance together.

title: 海景房 describes panoramic case styling
content: 海景房 normally means a panoramic tempered-glass case with strong component visibility. It is a case-style preference, not a performance or form-factor requirement.

title: Upgradeability is a platform-level preference
content: 方便升级 or future-proof should affect socket longevity, motherboard expansion, PSU headroom, case clearance, memory slots, and storage slots. It must not be reduced to one part tag.

title: 4K high refresh is a target, not a guaranteed benchmark
content: A request such as 4K 240Hz expresses a display and workload target. Actual frame rate varies by game, settings, ray tracing, and upscaling, so the system must not promise 240 FPS in every title.
